item.title: ESG is dead
item.description: Long live ESG

item.title: Conflicts of interest are back — and more blatant than ever
item.description: Democrats would do well to focus their fire on the use of public office by Donald Trump’s circle for private gain

item.title: The perks of parenting by spreadsheet
item.description: One simple trick to share it equally

item.title: Takanomics vs Abenomics: the $2.3tn prizefight
item.description: Japan’s new prime minister has broken with her mentor by encouraging firms to invest according to a government plan

item.title: The rating reforms needed to tackle private credit’s vulnerabilities
item.description: Once again rating agencies are the centre of systemic concerns

item.title: Renewed Hormuz tensions raise chances of a pricey winter
item.description: Asia and Europe would end up competing for LNG cargoes during their cold season if market is short, pushing up prices

item.title: IBM’s profit warning shows tech valuations are all in the timing
item.description: There is a case for believing AI revenue will grow more slowly than the hyperscalers have baked into their plans

item.title: Ukraine’s self-defeating reshuffle
item.description: Sacking of the defence minister is a setback for the country’s military efforts

item.title: PayPal investors can afford not to check out too hastily
item.description: Advent International and Stripe’s $53bn offer is a bargain for the buyers

item.title: The UK finally has a blueprint for innovation — now it must act on it
item.description: The Whittle Lab aims to transform aerothermal engineering, one of the country’s strongest manufacturing industries

item.title: The UK is forgetting the dull bit of climate change policy
item.description: Adaptation has never been the priority, but this summer’s heat is a message that the Treasury needs to act

item.title: What Wisconsin socialists tell us about the Democratic fightback
item.description: The gubernatorial primary is a test bed for a radical pitch to voters in Midwest Trump country

item.title: One answer to EU strategic autonomy is orbiting overhead
item.description: The Galileo satellite programme offers a model for funding joint defence projects and creating joint revenues

item.title: A new economic order will be built piece by ad hoc piece
item.description: Traditional trade deals will not address geopolitical concerns

item.title: IBM’s share plunge is a warning on AI
item.description: AI demand is starting to crowd out other forms of sector spending

item.title: Wall Street banks are AI stocks now
item.description: Plus a nice cool CPI report

item.title: Is the US or Europe better off?
item.description: Comparing the two economies is harder than it seems

item.title: Trump is driving another nail into the coffin of US science
item.description: Under proposed reforms, political appointees will review grants to ensure they “advance the President’s priorities”

item.title: Is Rightmove taking us for a ride?
item.description: Estate agents are convinced the UK’s most popular property portal exploits its dominant position — but determining whether vendors foot the bill is tricky

item.title: Paramount and Disney are chasing a pre-stream pipe dream
item.description: Studios built streaming platforms believing it would create a tight bond with viewers

item.title: Explain it to me like I’m 10
item.description: Simplifying how we explain complex concepts can lead to surprising benefits

item.title: Will I be protected from unfair dismissal in six months?
item.description: I’ve just started a new job after a rocky work history and want to check my employment rights

item.title: Britain does not need looser fiscal rules. It needs better ones
item.description: The budget framework needs to be fixed to reward stewardship rather than neglect

item.title: Britain’s electric air taxi contender needs more power
item.description: US hedge fund that controls Vertical Aerospace wants a strategic partner to boost its credibility

item.title: Brinkmanship in the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: America’s president is trapped, but Tehran is overplaying its hand
